definition: Meta's developer-facing API that lets medium and large businesses send and receive WhatsApp messages at scale. Since the on-premise API was sunset in October 2025, the Cloud API (hosted by Meta) is now the only option. Businesses access it either directly through Meta's API or through a Business Solution Provider (BSP) like Twilio, 360dialog, or Respond.io. It powers automation, CRM integrations, multi-agent inboxes, template-based outbound campaigns, and chatbot workflows.
target_audience: Mid-size to enterprise businesses that need to handle high message volumes, run marketing campaigns, operate multi-agent support teams, or integrate WhatsApp into existing CRM/helpdesk systems. Also for ecommerce stores (WooCommerce, Shopify) that want automated order notifications, cart recovery, and catalog sync. Requires either a developer on staff or a BSP platform to manage. Not practical for a solo business owner with no technical support.
key_features: Unlimited agents through shared inbox platforms (via BSPs)
Template-based outbound messaging for marketing, utility, and authentication use cases
Full automation support: chatbot flows, conditional logic, WhatsApp Flows (in-chat forms, surveys, bookings)
CRM and helpdesk integrations (HubSpot, Salesforce, Zendesk, Freshdesk, etc.) through BSPs or direct API connection
Product catalog with potential WooCommerce/Shopify sync through BSPs like Interakt, Zoko, or AiSensy
Broadcast messaging to thousands or hundreds of thousands of contacts per day (tier-dependent)
Click-to-WhatsApp Ads (CTWA) with 72-hour free messaging window and CAPI conversion tracking
WhatsApp Coexistence (2025): can run alongside the Business App on the same number
Throughput of up to 1,000 messages per second on Cloud API (4x the old on-premise API)
Portfolio-level messaging limits (post-October 2025) shared across all numbers in a business portfolio
limitations: Not free -- per-message fees from Meta plus BSP subscription/markup costs
Requires technical setup: either direct API integration (developer needed) or onboarding through a BSP platform
Template messages must be pre-approved by Meta before sending, with a review process of up to 24 hours
Marketing messages are subject to a frequency cap of approximately 2 per user per day
Quality rating system (Green/Yellow/Red) can restrict your messaging limits if customers report or block your messages
General-purpose AI chatbots banned since January 15, 2026 -- only structured, purpose-driven bots allowed
No native UI -- you need a BSP dashboard or custom-built interface to actually use it
Coexistence feature not available in EU/EEA, UK, Australia, Japan, and several other countries
Template misclassification by Meta's auto-review can result in higher fees or rejection
pricing: As of July 1, 2025, Meta moved from conversation-based to per-message pricing. You're charged per delivered template message, with rates varying by category and recipient country. Service/customer-initiated messages (replies within the 24-hour window) are free. Utility templates sent within the 24-hour service window are also free. Marketing messages: $0.0107 (India) to $0.1365 (parts of the Middle East), with common rates around $0.025-$0.065 for major markets (US ~$0.025, UK ~$0.0529, Brazil ~$0.0625). Utility messages: $0.004-$0.0456 depending on country. Authentication messages: $0.004-$0.0456. Volume-based pricing tiers offer lower rates at higher volumes for utility and authentication. CTWA-initiated conversations get a 72-hour completely free window. On top of Meta's fees, BSPs charge their own fees: monthly subscriptions typically range from $50-$500+/month depending on the platform, and some add a per-message markup (e.g., Twilio adds $0.005 per message). The Cloud API itself has no hosting costs since Meta runs the infrastructure, which is a major cost reduction from the old on-premise API.
scalability_ceiling: The API scales far beyond what the app can do. Messaging limits work on a tier system managed at the portfolio level (since October 2025): Tier 0 starts at 250 unique contacts per 24 hours, then scales to 1K, 10K, and 100K. Meta is removing the 2K and 10K tiers in Q1-Q2 2026, so verified businesses will jump straight to 100K/day after Business Verification. Unlimited tier is available for businesses consistently sending 50K-100K messages per day over 7 days. Tier upgrades are evaluated every 6 hours. Throughput caps at 1,000 messages per second on Cloud API. For most businesses, the API's limits won't be the bottleneck -- cost and quality management will be.
setup_complexity: 3 out of 5 via BSP, 4-5 out of 5 for direct API integration. The BSP route: sign up with a provider (Twilio, Respond.io, Wati, etc.), complete Meta's Embedded Signup flow, verify your business in Meta Business Manager, register your phone number, and start sending. This takes 1-3 days for a typical WordPress site owner using a BSP. Direct API integration requires a developer to handle webhooks, message sending/receiving, template management, and error handling -- realistically a 1-2 week project. Both paths require Meta Business Verification (submitting legal business documents), which can take a few days to a couple of weeks.
migration_complexity: Migrating from the Business App to Cloud API has gotten significantly easier with the 2025 Coexistence feature. In supported regions, you can onboard your existing app number to the Cloud API through a BSP without deleting the app. Chat history from the last ~6 months is preserved, and both platforms work simultaneously. For unsupported regions (EU/EEA, UK, etc.), you still need to register the number on a new WABA, which means losing chat history. Number porting between BSPs takes minutes with near-zero downtime -- the source WABA keeps working until the destination completes registration. Template messages don't migrate between WABAs and need to be recreated and re-approved. The Coexistence model means you no longer have to choose one or the other in most markets.
data_compliance: The Cloud API is significantly better for GDPR compliance than the Business App. Key advantages: no phone book access (only processes data of opted-in customers), Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) available through Meta and BSPs, ability to route through EU-based BSPs with EU data hosting, and proper opt-in/consent management tools. Businesses must still implement double opt-in for GDPR compliance and maintain records of consent. Since 2026, AI interactions in WhatsApp must be labeled under the EU AI Act (mandatory labeling, with transition periods through 2026). BSPs like Chatarmin and 360dialog offer EU-hosted infrastructure specifically for GDPR-sensitive businesses. Meta's Cloud API infrastructure is primarily US-based, but the BSP layer can provide EU data residency for data at rest.
verification_path: The API unlocks the full Official Business Account (OBA) verification path. Requirements: WhatsApp Business Account (WABA) must be 30+ days old, Meta Business Portfolio must complete Business Verification, two-step verification enabled, display name approved and matching external branding, and the business must be 'notable' (3-5 organic press articles in reputable media). OBA verification must be submitted through a BSP -- businesses can no longer apply directly. Timeline: roughly 6-10 weeks total (1 week API approval + 4-6 weeks quality history + 2-4 weeks OBA review). If rejected, you can reapply after a 30-day cooldown. Alternatively, Meta Verified subscription is available for instant verification without notability requirements, but costs $12-21+/month.
message_template_rules: All outbound messages outside the 24-hour customer service window must use pre-approved templates. Templates fall into three categories: Marketing (promotions, upsells, cross-sells), Utility (order updates, shipping alerts, appointment reminders), and Authentication (OTPs, verification codes). Each template must be submitted through WhatsApp Manager or your BSP for review, which takes up to 24 hours. Meta may auto-reclassify templates that don't match their stated category, which affects pricing. Duplicate templates with identical body/footer text are rejected (except authentication). Misclassification can result in higher charges (a utility template reclassified as marketing costs more). Templates support variables, buttons, headers with images/documents, and footer text. This is a meaningful operational overhead that the Business App doesn't have.
messaging_limits: Since October 7, 2025, messaging limits are managed at the business portfolio level (shared across all phone numbers in the portfolio). Tier structure: 250 unique contacts/24h (new/unverified), then 1K, 10K, 100K. Meta is removing the 2K and 10K tiers in Q1-Q2 2026 -- verified businesses will jump to 100K/day. Unlimited tier requires consistently sending 50K-100K messages/day over 7 days. Tier upgrades are evaluated every 6 hours. Marketing messages have an additional frequency cap of approximately 2 marketing template messages per user per day. Quality rating must stay Green or Yellow for tier upgrades -- Red quality or Flagged status freezes your tier and can trigger a downgrade after 7 days.
ai_automation_policy: As of January 15, 2026, Meta banned general-purpose AI chatbots from WhatsApp. This means no open-domain AI assistants, no ChatGPT-style bots, no bots that answer arbitrary questions or generate content. What's still allowed: structured, purpose-driven bots for customer support, order tracking, appointment booking, FAQ answering, and sales workflows. A travel agency bot that answers customer questions about bookings is fine. A general AI assistant that can discuss any topic is not. This is enforced at the API level and has been challenged by regulators (Brazil's CADE tried to block it but Meta won the appeal in January 2026). Businesses using AI incidentally within structured workflows are unaffected.
quality_risk: The API uses a formal quality rating system: Green (high quality), Yellow (medium), Red (low). Ratings are based on customer feedback over the past 7 days -- blocks, reports, and other negative signals. If your rating drops to Red, your status changes to 'Flagged,' which prevents tier upgrades. If quality doesn't improve within 7 days, your messaging tier is downgraded. Persistent poor quality can lead to template restrictions (7-30 day bans on specific templates) or account suspension. Recovery from a blocked API account requires submitting an appeal to Meta, which takes 3-10 business days. If the appeal fails, the number is permanently banned with no recovery option. Best practices: only message opted-in contacts, personalize messages, keep frequency reasonable, and monitor your quality dashboard regularly.
ads_integration: Full CTWA (Click-to-WhatsApp Ads) support with the 72-hour free messaging window. When a customer clicks your Facebook or Instagram ad and opens a WhatsApp conversation, all messages within the next 72 hours are completely free -- including marketing templates. This makes CTWA campaigns extremely cost-effective for lead generation. CAPI (Conversions API) integration is available through BSPs, enabling server-to-server conversion tracking. You can fire events like 'Demo Booked,' 'Purchase Made,' or 'Lead Qualified' back to Meta Ads Manager for campaign optimization. This is critical for measuring WhatsApp campaign ROI since there's no browser pixel involved. CAPI requires a BSP with the integration built in (Wati, AiSensy, Infobip, etc.).
ecommerce_features: The API supports product catalogs that can be displayed in-chat, with the ability to sync with WooCommerce and Shopify through BSPs like Interakt, Zoko, and AiSensy. These integrations enable automated order confirmations, shipping updates, abandoned cart recovery messages, and back-in-stock alerts via utility templates. WhatsApp Flows allow customers to complete purchases, fill out forms, and select product variants directly within the chat without leaving WhatsApp. In-chat payments are available in select markets (India, Brazil). For WooCommerce store owners, the BSP integration can sync your product catalog, trigger automated messages based on order status changes, and even handle customer support queries related to specific orders.
wpchat_relevance: WPChat.com complements the API by handling the website-side customer entry point. While the API handles the backend messaging infrastructure, WPChat provides the front-end chat widget that lives on your WordPress site. For API users, WPChat adds: a branded chat widget that routes visitors to your WhatsApp Business number, AI-powered FAQs that deflect common questions before they hit your API-powered inbox (saving per-message costs), Chat Funnels that qualify leads before they enter your support queue, and agent availability scheduling that shows the right team members at the right time. The key value for API users is cost optimization -- every question answered by WPChat's on-site FAQs is a conversation that doesn't cost you a template message or agent time on the API side.
best_for: Mid-size businesses handling 50+ daily WhatsApp conversations
Ecommerce stores (WooCommerce/Shopify) that want automated order notifications and cart recovery
Companies running multi-agent customer support teams
Marketing teams running WhatsApp broadcast campaigns to thousands of contacts
Businesses that need CRM integration (HubSpot, Salesforce, etc.) with WhatsApp
Agencies managing WhatsApp communication for multiple clients
Enterprises requiring GDPR-compliant WhatsApp communication with DPAs and EU data hosting
not_suited_for: Solo business owners with low message volume who don't need automation or integrations. If you're handling fewer than 20-30 conversations per day and don't need CRM sync, the API's cost and complexity aren't justified. The Business App (possibly with WPChat on your site) will do everything you need.
cta_angle: Running the WhatsApp Business API on your WordPress site? WPChat.com handles the piece the API doesn't -- the on-site experience. It captures visitors, qualifies them through Chat Funnels, and answers basic questions with AI FAQs before they ever hit your API-powered inbox. That means lower message costs and better-qualified leads flowing into your support or sales team.
